calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Continued To Feb-05-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 403

MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL

Appearances:
Susan M. Carson of the Office of the Attorney General (703.5580) for respondent and opposing party
Jordan Keville of Hooper Lundy & Bookman (310.551.8103) for petitioner and moving party

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: parties are to submit further briefing by February 18, 2009, at which time the matter will be taken under submission. Briefs are to address the following questions: 1) Is there a substantial evidentiary analysis related to the 2003 enactment by the legislature that meets the standards set forth in the Minnesota case? Parties are limited to referencing what was in the record at the time the writ was decided and, to the extent that the parties reference something specific, it should be attached as a supplement. 2) Is there legal authority for a new trial motion based solely on new legal theory absent a demonstration that there is an undisputed set of facts in the record that would support the new legal theory? (403/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR: 8600

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: PETITIONER CALIFORNIA HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to September 11, 2008. By September 3, 2008 parties are to provide the Court, either jointly or separately, with the text of the Boren Amendment, text of the regulations that presently apply, and Section 30(A). NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO MAY 28, 2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES AND SANDRA SHEWRY, DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.
APPEARANCES: JORDAN B. KEVILLE, ESQUIRE AND LLOYD A. BOOKMAN, ESQUIRE (HOOPER LUNDY & BOOKMAN, 310.551.8103) APPEARING FOR PETITIONER. SUSAN CARSON, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.703.5580) APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING: The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. In ruling on the motion, the Court construes the allegations liberally to determine if there is a basis for liability. The complaint is replete with allegations that the State's methodology is "unsupported, arbitrary and unreasonable." See paragraphs 2, 3, 5 and 22. The complaint also pleads facts that, if proven, would support such allegations. See paragraphs 31-35. Plaintiff also pleads a beneficial interest in paragraphs 38 and 39. Waste Management of Alameda County, Inc. v. County of Alameda (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1233.
"Although traditional mandamus will not lie to control discretion of a public official or agency, that is, to force the exercise of discretion in a particular manner, it will lie to correct abuses of discretion, and will lie to force a particular action by the officer, when the law clearly establishes the petitioner's right to such action." Miller Family Home, Inc. v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 488, 491.
The absence of an enforceable right under section 1983 does not preclude relief under CCP section 1085. California Homeless & Housing Coalition v. Anderson (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 450, 458. PETITIONER TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
